{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} == Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==

Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. {{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} == Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} == Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>

Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} == Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>

Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} == Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine

revision.comment: Text replacement - "皇大神宮}}" to "皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine

Ise|おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]]

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

[[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==

[[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] <references />{{125 Shrines of Ise|おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

[[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] <references />{{125 Shrines of Ise|おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|神宮125社の一覧" to "|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧"
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] <references />{{125 Shrines of Ise|おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi no Kami), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] <references /> [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}{{125 Shrines of Ise}}

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] <references /> [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:to be resorted]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
