[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] <references /> [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:to be resorted]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}}

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=draft}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|12=draft}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=draft}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine
== References ==

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=draft}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|12=draft}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=draft}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Refreshing imported pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|12=draft|12=draft}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=draft|12=draft}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=draft|12=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=draft|12=draft}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大山祇神社 (伊勢市) up to rev 98956129
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 |画像= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= [[皇大神宮]]所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまつみじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の所管社。内宮の宮域内、[[宇治橋 (伊勢市)|宇治橋]]東詰から東に進むと[[神宮衛士|衛士]]見張所があり、見張所付近に[[子安神社 (伊勢市)|子安神社]]と並び鎮座する<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ</ref>。
== 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]宇治館町の伊勢神宮内宮境内に鎮座する<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：16ページ</ref>。[[神路山]]の山裾にある<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：55ページ</ref>。内宮の所管社30社のうち第29位である<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：15 - 16ページ</ref>。旧称を「山神社」と言い、[[神宮式年遷宮]]の最初の祭儀である「山口祭」はこの[[神社]]で催行されていたとされる<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。その後、[[杣|御杣山]]の移動に伴い、山口祭の祭場が移動し、大山祇神社に捧げられることはなくなった<ref name="ic"/>。
[[社殿]]は[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。伊勢神宮の摂社・末社・所管社には通例、[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない<ref>伊勢文化舎（2008）：26ページ</ref>が、大山祇神社には賽銭箱が置かれている。社殿の手前には[[花]]の形をした[[手水鉢]]がある<ref name="ic"/>。その手前にあるのが子安神社、その奥にあるのが大山祇神社<ref name="ic"/>。
[[祭神]]は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]（おおやまつみのかみ）<ref name="ic"/>。[[イザナギ|伊弉諾命]]（いざなぎのみこと）・[[イザナミ|伊弉冉命]]（いざなみのみこと）の子であり、山の守護神とされる<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。
== 歴史 == 創建は不詳。ただし『[[延喜式神名帳]]』および『延暦儀式帳』に記載がないことから、古代には存在しなかったと推察される<ref name="st">櫻井（1991）：266, 272ページ</ref>。[[明治5年]]6月（グレゴリオ暦：[[1872年]]7月）、[[教部省]]は両[[古書]]に記載のない神社を一律に神宮所管から外し、[[度会県]]および三重県管轄に移行したため、大山祇神社も神宮所管から離れることとなった<ref name="st"/>。県の所管に移行した大山祇神社は[[1879年]]（明治12年）以降、地元・宇治館町の[[産土神]]となった<ref name="st1">櫻井（1991）：273ページ</ref>。
一方の神宮側では、内宮宮域にある大山祇神社と外宮宮域にある[[茜社]]（あこねやしろ）を神宮の所管に復帰すべく、[[神社局]]と内々に折衝を開始した<ref name="st1"/>。これは、両社が神苑の中央付近にあり、神宮にとって重要な場所であることと、紅白の[[幟]]や傷んだ[[鳥居]]などが林立する光景が神苑の[[景観]]を損ねているという理由からであった<ref name="st1"/>。茜社の[[氏子]]は神宮復帰を拒否したが、大山祇神社の氏子は[[1900年]]（明治33年）に自ら神宮に神宮所管への復帰を願い出た<ref name="st2">櫻井（1991）：273 - 274ページ</ref>。（実際には神宮司庁からの要請により願い出たようである<ref name="st2"/>。）この願いが受け入れられ、大山祇神社とその境内社の子安神社および両社の社地174余[[坪]]（≒575[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）が1900年（明治33年）、内宮に編入された<ref>櫻井（1991）：274ページ</ref>。
[[1924年]]（大正11年）3月に造り替えられた<ref name="uy"/>。
== 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]][[伊勢市駅]]南口（JR側）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、内宮前下車<ref name="mkr">三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2013年9月12日閲覧。）</ref>。近鉄鳥羽線[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に[[駐車場]]あり<ref name="mkr"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html 大山祇神社（おおやまつみじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 |画像= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= [[皇大神宮]]所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまつみじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の所管社。内宮の宮域内、[[宇治橋 (伊勢市)|宇治橋]]東詰から東に進むと[[神宮衛士|衛士]]見張所があり、見張所付近に[[子安神社 (伊勢市)|子安神社]]と並び鎮座する<ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ</ref>。
== 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]宇治館町の伊勢神宮内宮境内に鎮座する<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：16ページ</ref>。[[神路山]]の山裾にある<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：55ページ</ref>。内宮の所管社30社のうち第29位である<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：15 - 16ページ</ref>。旧称を「山神社」と言い、[[神宮式年遷宮]]の最初の祭儀である「山口祭」はこの[[神社]]で催行されていたとされる<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。その後、[[杣|御杣山]]の移動に伴い、山口祭の祭場が移動し、大山祇神社に捧げられることはなくなった<ref name="ic"/>。
[[社殿]]は[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。伊勢神宮の摂社・末社・所管社には通例、[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない<ref>伊勢文化舎（2008）：26ページ</ref>が、大山祇神社には賽銭箱が置かれている。社殿の手前には[[花]]の形をした[[手水鉢]]がある<ref name="ic"/>。その手前にあるのが子安神社、その奥にあるのが大山祇神社<ref name="ic"/>。
[[祭神]]は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]（おおやまつみのかみ）<ref name="ic"/>。[[イザナギ|伊弉諾命]]（いざなぎのみこと）・[[イザナミ|伊弉冉命]]（いざなみのみこと）の子であり、山の守護神とされる<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>。
== 歴史 == 創建は不詳。ただし『[[延喜式神名帳]]』および『延暦儀式帳』に記載がないことから、古代には存在しなかったと推察される<ref name="st">櫻井（1991）：266, 272ページ</ref>。[[明治5年]]6月（グレゴリオ暦：[[1872年]]7月）、[[教部省]]は両[[古書]]に記載のない神社を一律に神宮所管から外し、[[度会県]]および三重県管轄に移行したため、大山祇神社も神宮所管から離れることとなった<ref name="st"/>。県の所管に移行した大山祇神社は[[1879年]]（明治12年）以降、地元・宇治館町の[[産土神]]となった<ref name="st1">櫻井（1991）：273ページ</ref>。
一方の神宮側では、内宮宮域にある大山祇神社と外宮宮域にある[[茜社]]（あこねやしろ）を神宮の所管に復帰すべく、[[神社局]]と内々に折衝を開始した<ref name="st1"/>。これは、両社が神苑の中央付近にあり、神宮にとって重要な場所であることと、紅白の[[幟]]や傷んだ[[鳥居]]などが林立する光景が神苑の[[景観]]を損ねているという理由からであった<ref name="st1"/>。茜社の[[氏子]]は神宮復帰を拒否したが、大山祇神社の氏子は[[1900年]]（明治33年）に自ら神宮に神宮所管への復帰を願い出た<ref name="st2">櫻井（1991）：273 - 274ページ</ref>。（実際には神宮司庁からの要請により願い出たようである<ref name="st2"/>。）この願いが受け入れられ、大山祇神社とその境内社の子安神社および両社の社地174余[[坪]]（≒575[[平方メートル|m<sup>2</sup>]]）が1900年（明治33年）、内宮に編入された<ref>櫻井（1991）：274ページ</ref>。
[[1924年]]（大正11年）3月に造り替えられた<ref name="uy"/>。
== 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]][[伊勢市駅]]南口（JR側）または近鉄山田線・[[近鉄鳥羽線|鳥羽線]][[宇治山田駅]]より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、内宮前下車<ref name="mkr">三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2013年9月12日閲覧。）</ref>。近鉄鳥羽線[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に[[駐車場]]あり<ref name="mkr"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)}} {{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|version=98956129|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }}
[[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]]

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiji-yama|qq=}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka Publishing|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Uji Bridge (Ise)]] → [[Uji Bridge (Ise City)]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiji-yama|qq=}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka Publishing|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine

{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiji-yama|qq=}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka Publishing|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine
== References ==

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg|270px]] |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiji-yama|qq=}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka Publishing|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine
== References ==
<references /> {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|version=98956129|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223627]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.458361|N|136.725083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiji-yama|qq=}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka Publishing|qq=}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine

[[sn:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] [[ja:大山祇神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Oyamatsumi-jinja (Ise)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223627]] Wikidata name: Ōyamatsumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.458361|N|136.725083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiji-yama|qq=|1=Mount Kamiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590896}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>

The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>

* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|qq=|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka Publishing|qq=|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
