{{Infobox religious building |名称= 大山祇神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Oyamazumi Shrine, Ise.jpg |所在地= 三重県伊勢市宇治館町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 30.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 30.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= 不詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 山神社 |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.458361|N|136.725083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine). Located within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikatamachi, Ise City, Mie Prefecture, it stands alongside {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} near the guard station east of {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋 (伊勢市)|lt=Uji Bridge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.115</ref>
== Overview ==
Ōyamazumi Shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Kamiji-yama|ja|神路山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiji-yama|1=Mount Kamiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590896}} within the grounds of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.16</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref> It Ranks 29th among the 30 auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.15-16</ref> Formerly known as "Yama Shrine" (山神社), it was the site of the "Yamaguchi Festival," the first ritual of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> Later, as the sacred forest (御杣山) was relocated, the festival site moved, and offerings ceased at Ōyamazumi Shrine.<ref name="ic"/>
The shrine is enclosed by a tamagaki (sacred fence). Unlike most auxiliary shrines under Ise Grand Shrine, Ōyamazumi Shrine has a saisenbako (offertory box). In front of the main hall, there is a floral-shaped chozubachi (water basin).<ref name="ic"/>
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} (Ōyamatsumi), considered the guardian deity of mountains and the child of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The founding date is unknown. As it is not mentioned in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" or the "Enryaku Gishikichō," it is presumed that the shrine did not exist in ancient times.<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient records from Ise Grand Shrine's jurisdiction, transferring them to Watarai Prefecture and Mie Prefecture.<ref name="st"/>
By 1879, Ōyamazumi Shrine became the tutelary deity of Ujikatamachi.<ref name="st1">Sakurai (1991): p.273</ref> However, efforts by the shrine office led to its re-incorporation into Ise Grand Shrine in 1900, along with {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|lt=Koyasu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.<ref name="st2">Sakurai (1991): pp.273-274</ref>
The shrine was reconstructed in March 1924.<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
* **By Train:** JR Sangu Line / Kintetsu Yamada Line - Ise-shi Station (JR side) or Ujiyamada Station, then approximately 15 minutes by Mie Kotsu bus to Naiku-mae bus stop.<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Naiku) Tourist Facilities & Surroundings - Mie Sightseeing]" (accessed September 12, 2013)</ref> * **By Car:** About 5 minutes south from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 or National Route 23. Parking is available nearby.<ref name="mkr"/>
== References ==
* Ujiyamada City Office. *Ujiyamada City History, Volume 1*. Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}}. *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine*. Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * Katsunoshin Sakurai. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine*. Kokushokankōkai, November 30, 1991, 318 pages. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * *Visiting the 125 Shrines of Ise*. Special Edition of *Isejin*, {{ill|Ise Bunka Publishing|ja|伊勢文化舎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Bunka Publishing|1=Ise Bunka-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379080}}, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0105.html Ōyamazumi Shrine] - Foundation for the Veneration of Ise Grand Shrine
== References ==
<references /> {{125 Shrines of Ise}}
